questions.questionText: The hallmark of iron deficiency anemia in a peripheral blood smear is:
questions.explanation: Lack of iron impairs hemoglobin synthesis, producing small, pale RBCs.

questions.questionText: Megaloblastic anemia is most commonly caused by a deficiency of:
questions.explanation: Impaired DNA synthesis leads to large, immature RBC precursors (megaloblasts).

questions.questionText: The finding of Heinz bodies and bite cells in a peripheral smear is characteristic of:
questions.explanation: Oxidized, denatured hemoglobin forms Heinz bodies, which are 'bitten' out by splenic macrophages.

questions.questionText: A substitution of valine for glutamic acid at the 6th position of the beta-globin chain results in:
questions.explanation: This point mutation leads to polymerization of deoxygenated HbS.

questions.questionText: Reed-Sternberg cells are the pathognomonic finding in:
questions.explanation: These are large, binucleated 'owl-eye' cells characteristic of Classic Hodgkin lymphoma.

questions.questionText: The Philadelphia chromosome [t(9;22)] forming the BCR-ABL fusion gene is the hallmark of:
questions.explanation: The BCR-ABL fusion protein has constitutive tyrosine kinase activity.

questions.questionText: Auer rods are cytoplasmic inclusions typically found in the blast cells of:
questions.explanation: Auer rods represent fused lysosomes/granules, highly specific for AML.

questions.questionText: Bence Jones proteinuria is classically associated with:
questions.explanation: Bence Jones proteins are free immunoglobulin light chains excreted in urine.

questions.questionText: The characteristic translocation in Burkitt lymphoma is:
questions.explanation: Translocation of c-myc directly behind the Ig heavy chain promoter drives proliferation.

questions.questionText: Which coagulation test is characteristically prolonged in Hemophilia A?
questions.explanation: Hemophilia A (Factor VIII deficiency) affects the intrinsic pathway, tested by aPTT.

questions.questionText: In Hematopathology, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Pathology texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Pathology references for Hematopathology. Subject: Pathology. Chapter: Hematopathology.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Hematopathology scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Pathology. Chapter: Hematopathology.
